analysis.title: We’re reviewing your answers
analysis.subtitle: This will only take a moment

intro.title: Before you start
intro.description: This helps you understand possible anxiety patterns in a simple way. It’s not a diagnosis.

points.honestAnswers: Answer based on your real experience — not what sounds more serious.
points.safety: If you feel unsafe right now, reach out for real-world support.
points.result: You’ll get a short, clear explanation — not a clinical report.

panicSymptomLabels.chokingSensations: Feeling like you can’t breathe or are choking

gadStep.title: Ongoing worry
gadStep.description: These questions look at the past 2 weeks, and whether this pattern has been around longer.

gad7ItemQuestions.duration: Has this kind of worry been going on for 6 months or more?
gad7ItemQuestions.feelingNervousOrOnEdge: Have you often felt tense or on edge?
gad7ItemQuestions.difficultyControllingWorry: Was it hard to stop or control your worrying?
gad7ItemQuestions.worryingTooMuchAboutDifferentThings: Did you worry a lot about different things?
gad7ItemQuestions.troubleRelaxing: Was it hard to relax or switch off?
gad7ItemQuestions.restlessness: Did you feel so restless that it was hard to sit still?
gad7ItemQuestions.irritability: Did you get easily irritated or annoyed?
gad7ItemQuestions.fearThatSomethingAwfulMayHappen: Did you often feel like something bad might happen?

panicStep.title: Sudden anxiety spikes
panicStep.description: This looks at moments where fear or anxiety rises quickly.

questions.hadSurges: In the past month, did you have sudden spikes of strong fear or anxiety?
questions.peakWithinMinutes: Did they build quickly and peak within a few minutes?
questions.symptomsTitle: At the most intense moment, what did you notice?
questions.persistentConcern: Afterwards, did you keep worrying about it happening again?
questions.behaviorChange: Did you change your behavior to avoid it?

maintainerStep.title: What keeps it going
maintainerStep.description: Some habits can keep anxiety going — even if they’re meant to help.

worryLoop.title: Worry loop
worryLoop.description: How much do these show up in your daily life?

panicLoop.title: Body alarm loop
panicLoop.description: These habits can make sudden fear feel unfinished or more intense.

panicMaintainerLabels.bodyScanning: I scan my body for signs that anxiety is starting
panicMaintainerLabels.carryingSafetyItems: I keep things with me mainly to feel safe (water, meds, phone)
panicMaintainerLabels.escapePlanning: I think about how to leave quickly if anxiety rises
panicMaintainerLabels.avoidingPhysicalExertion: I avoid activities or sensations that might feel intense
panicMaintainerLabels.needingSafePersonOrExit: I feel safer only if I have a trusted person or an easy way out

worryMaintainerLabels.reassuranceSeeking: I ask others for reassurance to calm down
worryMaintainerLabels.checkingAndResearching: I repeatedly check or research things to feel certain
worryMaintainerLabels.overPreparing: I over-prepare to prevent things from going wrong
worryMaintainerLabels.avoidingUncertainty: I avoid situations when the outcome feels uncertain
worryMaintainerLabels.mentalReviewing: I go over scenarios in my mind again and again

impairmentStep.title: Impact
impairmentStep.description: This looks at how much anxiety affects your daily life.

contextStep.title: Important context
contextStep.description: These help keep the result realistic.

questions.symptomsOnlyDuringSubstanceUse: Do these mainly happen after substance use?
questions.symptomsBetterExplainedByMedicalProblem: Could a medical condition explain this?
questions.recentTraumaCueDominant: Do these mostly happen around a recent stressful event?
questions.recentManiaLikeState: Have you had a recent period of very high energy and little sleep?
questions.currentlyFeelsUnsafe: Do you feel unsafe right now?

previewStep.title: Review your answers
previewStep.description: This is a cautious preview — not a diagnosis.

panic.needingSafePersonOrExit: needing a safe person or exit

actions.title: What to focus on
actions.description: These are general directions — not a treatment plan.
